  • Internals and Porting Notes
    • Available internals pages
  • diCal2 Native Parity Notes
    • Why these changes were necessary
    • What changed
      • 1. Shared upstream/native result normalization
      • 2. VCF reader now preserves upstream-style physical-locus metadata
      • 3. Grouped-locus counting now uses physical blocks, not just segregating columns
      • 4. Refined intervals now materialize interval-specific epochs
      • 5. Meta-start generation was tightened toward upstream semantics
      • 6. Gallery rerun used a targeted diCal2-only render path
      • 7. Meta-start replay tracing is now available on the native path
    • What improved
    • What still does not match
    • Next steps
  • PSMC Internals
    • Why this page exists
    • Source map
    • Core model
    • The parts that matter for parity
    • What stayed out of the main docs
  • SMC++ Internals
    • Why this page exists
    • Core ingredients
    • Porting hotspots
    • Current native state
    • Handoff notes
    • Upstream bridge notes
    • Where to read code
    • What stayed out of the main docs
  • SMC++ Parity Closure Notes
    • Scope
    • Final state
    • What was actually wrong
      • 1. Native one-pop preprocessing was not phase-aligned with upstream
      • 2. Native one-pop HMM was leaving the upstream compressed-row ceremony
    • How the issue was found
    • The fixes that closed parity
      • Upstream preprocessing offset in native one-pop preprocessing
      • Stay on the compressed run-length stream in the one-pop HMM
    • Earlier fixes that mattered
      • One-pop Watterson estimator
      • Monomorphic one-pop emission handling
      • One-pop transition details
      • Prefit / initialization parity
      • Scale-step optimizer semantics
    • What did not close parity by itself
    • Practical lesson
    • Code pointers
  • eSMC2 Internals
    • Why this page exists
    • Core model additions over PSMC
    • Practical parity notes
    • Upstream bridge
    • Where to read code
    • What stayed out of the main docs
  • MSMC-IM Internals

smckit
  • Algorithm Map for Agents
  • View page source

Algorithm Map for Agents

PSMC

  • Purpose: infer N_e(t) from one diploid genome.

  • Input: .psmcfa.

  • Upstream status: vendored C source plus public upstream runner.

  • Native status: working and validated against tracked reference output.

  • Main outputs: time, lambda_k, ne, theta, rho.

MSMC2

  • Purpose: infer piecewise coalescence rates from multiple haplotypes.

  • Input: multihetsep.

  • Upstream status: vendored D source plus public CLI-backed upstream runner.

  • Native status: strong fixture parity is tracked.

  • Main outputs: time boundaries, lambdas, mutation/recombination estimates.

MSMC-IM

  • Purpose: fit a continuous isolation-migration model on top of MSMC2 output.

  • Input: MSMC2 combined output.

  • Upstream status: vendored Python script plus public upstream runner.

  • Native status: interchangeable with upstream on the enforced 4-case Yoruba/French oracle matrix, including the shared public payload fields.

  • Main outputs: N1, N2, m, M.

SMC++

  • Purpose: infer demography from many unphased individuals.

  • Input: .smc / .smc.gz.

  • Upstream status: vendored upstream source tree plus public bridge; execution still depends on a controlled side Python environment for the compiled runtime.

  • Native status: available; default native behavior now follows the upstream one-pop preprocessing, hidden-state, and compressed HMM path. The tracked one-pop matrix now clears strict parity on both the small control and the bundled larger .smc fixture, with fixed-model HMM statistics also matching upstream on that matrix.

  • Main outputs: ne, time, theta, rho, hidden-state metadata.

eSMC2

  • Purpose: extend pairwise SMC inference to dormancy and selfing.

  • Input: pairwise sequence / .psmcfa-style data.

  • Upstream status: vendored R package plus public bridge.

  • Native status: available; tracked fit parity is enforced on the current oracle fixture matrix, with upstream still acting as the broader fidelity baseline.

  • Main outputs: Tc, Xi, ne, beta, sigma, rho.

ASMC

  • Purpose: decode pairwise TMRCA along the genome at scale.

  • Input: ASMC haplotypes, maps, decoding quantities.

  • Upstream status: vendored source/data plus public executable-backed upstream runner.

  • Native status: good tracked parity on the vendored regression fixture.

  • Main outputs: posterior means, MAP states, aggregated posteriors.

diCal2

  • Purpose: fit structured demographic histories with migration and multiple populations.

  • Input: .param, .demo, .config, phased sequence data.

  • Upstream status: vendored diCal2.jar plus public stdout-parsing upstream bridge.

  • Native status: available with tracked oracle comparisons; README exp oracle-point and explicit-start parity are now close, but full meta-start interchangeability is still pending.

  • Main outputs: best-fit parameters, refined demography, likelihood metadata.
